title: Uniface and the Registry
text: Uniface and the Registry On Windows, Uniface saves logical printers, application layout state, command line history, and other settings in the Windows Registry. Default Registry Locations When a Uniface application is started, it first looks in an application-specific registry key for information, if there is one. If it can’t find it there, it looks in the default registry key. Default registry keys are stored under HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Uniface/Uniface Version / . Uniface stores logical printer definitions, command line history, and form layout state here. Application-specific registry keys are stored under HKEY_CURRENT_USER/Software/Uniface/Uniface Version / AppName . Installation information for Uniface 10 and Uniface Router are stored under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SOFTWARE/Uniface/Uniface Version / Application . COM options (set with the Uniface COM Configuration utility) are stored under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SOFTWARE/Uniface/Uniface Version / MyComDefinition . Note: When a Uniface 32-bit application is installed on a 64-bit machine, HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE definitions are stored under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE/SOFTWARE/Wow6432Node/Uniface/Uniface Version / Application . Editing the Registry You can use the $setting and deletesetting ProcScript instructions to read, write, and delete entries in the Registry. You can use the $setting and deletesetting Proc instructions to read, write, and delete entries in the Registry. CAUTION: Making changes to the Registry can significantly affect your computer, so use due care when using $setting and deletesetting . Uniface cannot be held responsible for changes you make to your Registry. Related Links $setting Retrieve, add, or change configuration settings. Depending on the platform, settings may be stored in initialization files, assignment files, environment variables, logicals, data areas, or the Windows Registry. deletesetting Delete initialization settings, environment variables, or runtime settings. Registry_Style Controls the position of logical printers in the Microsoft Windows registry. Configuring the COM Connector The COM connector provides full COM connectivity for Uniface applications.
